taxonomy: Rhamphorhynchus mendoncae is a species of pterosaur that belongs to the family Rhamphorhynchidae. This classification is based on its unique characteristics, such as its long fourth finger with a claw and a distinctive toothless beak. The Rhamphorhynchidae family is further grouped into the clade Pterosauria, which includes all flying reptiles. Within Pterosauria, Rhamphorhynchidae is part of the infraclass Archosauria, a larger group of reptiles that also includes crocodilians and birds. By understanding the classification of Rhamphorhynchus mendoncae, scientists can better comprehend its evolution and place in the history of life on Earth.
